formations_blocks: Ecuador using a direct counter/long-ball approach
Curaçao looking to attack in transition
adjustments: Ecuador repeatedly trying to draw Curaçao forward before playing over the top
Curaçao pressing after Ecuador's early backline mistake
key_matchups: Enner Valencia vs Curaçao center backs
Tahith Chong vs Ecuador's back line
Vite and Yeboah combining down Ecuador's right
phases.summary: The opening phase is defined by Ecuador trying to lure Curaçao up the pitch and then hit long balls over the top, which immediately created a major early chance for Enner Valencia. Curaçao are not passive: they press early, look confident in transition, and generate a few warning shots of their own, even if one key effort was ruled offside.

formations_blocks: Curaçao attempting to play through pressure rather than sit in a low block
adjustments: Moisés Caicedo dropping almost behind the center-backs to help build play and dictate tempo
Curaçao occasionally breaking pressure with line-breaking passes and quicker circulation
key_matchups: Moisés Caicedo vs Curaçao's first line of pressure
Gonzalo Plata vs Curaçao's defensive line on the edge of the box
Tahith Chong vs Ecuador's box defense
phases.summary: This phase was defined by Ecuador’s aggressive pressing game, which repeatedly forced Curaçao to battle through multiple waves of pressure just to advance the ball. Moisés Caicedo became the control point for Ecuador, at times dropping deep to orchestrate buildup, while Curaçao’s best moments came only when they managed to break the press and find space in the final third. Curaçao remained willing to attack rather than just sit deep, but Ecuador’s pressure largely dictated the flow.

adjustments: Ecuador made a half-time substitution: Kevin Rodríguez replaced Jordy Alcívar.
phases.summary: The half-time brief offers very little tactical detail beyond the match remaining goalless at the interval. The only concrete change mentioned is Ecuador's substitution at the restart, with Kevin Rodríguez coming on for Jordy Alcívar, while the sides also switched ends for the second half.

formations_blocks: Ecuador pushing high for an equalizer
Curaçao defending and countering from a deeper block
end-to-end transition phase after the restart
adjustments: Ecuador increasing attacking urgency and volume of shots
Curaçao absorbing pressure and breaking quickly on turnovers
set-piece pressure from Ecuador via free kicks and corners
key_matchups: Eloy Room vs Ecuador's aerial/set-piece pressure
Galindez vs Curaçao's counterattacking shots (Leandro Bacuna, Comenencia, Locadia)
Enner Valencia vs the Curaçao defensive setup
phases.summary: After the interval, Ecuador took control of territory and pressed harder for a goal, but their attacks became increasingly hurried and frustrated, with several speculative shots and a lack of clear ideas. Curaçao sat deeper, then repeatedly threatened in transition, forcing big saves from both Eloy Room and Galindez and turning the match into a chaotic end-to-end contest with high stakes for Ecuador.

formations_blocks: Curaçao limiting Ecuador’s opportunities with a disciplined defensive setup
Ecuador pressing around the edge of the box and looking to create a clear opening
adjustments: Tahith Chong introduced as a substitute
Juninho Bacuna stepping into shooting range at the top of the penalty area
key_matchups: Curaçao attack vs Ecuador’s chance suppression
Ecuador’s attacking quality vs Curaçao’s tenacity
phases.summary: The closing stages are defined by Curaçao’s stubborn resistance, with the commentary emphasizing their ability to limit Ecuador’s chances despite being clearly outmatched in talent. Curaçao then carve out a rare opening through Juninho Bacuna, whose shot from the top of the box is blocked by Galíndez, underscoring the contrast between Ecuador’s pressure and Curaçao’s limited but dangerous counter-threat.
